name: Coolify MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for Coolify — the open-source self-hosted PaaS (Heroku alternative). Enables AI agents to manage Coolify deployments, applications, databases, services, servers, and teams — automating the deployment lifecycle of self-hosted applications without vendor lock-in.
use_cases: Deploying and managing applications on Coolify from CI/CD and DevOps agents
Monitoring Coolify application status and deployment logs from operations agents
Managing Coolify database instances and services from infrastructure agents
Automating Coolify server provisioning and team management workflows
not_for: Teams using AWS, Render, Railway, or other managed PaaS (Coolify is self-hosted only)
Production deployments without proper Coolify server setup and backups
Teams preferring fully managed infrastructure over self-hosting
best_when: A developer or team self-hosting their stack with Coolify wants AI agents to automate deployments, manage services, and operate their Coolify PaaS through the MCP interface.
avoid_when: You use managed PaaS services. Coolify requires your own server with Coolify installed and running.

auth.notes: Coolify API token required. Generate in Coolify dashboard under Security > API Tokens.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — Coolify is open source self-hosted
pricing.paid_tiers: Coolify Cloud (managed hosting) is paid; self-hosted is free
pricing.notes: Coolify self-hosted is free open source (Apache 2.0). MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: No explicit rate limits — bounded by Coolify server performance

known_agent_gotchas: Deployment operations affect production applications — agents should confirm before deploying
Coolify API is evolving — check API version compatibility with your Coolify version
Server-level operations (restart, rebuild) can cause downtime — use with care in production
Community MCP server — may lag behind Coolify API updates
agent_readiness.security_notes: Coolify API token grants full infrastructure control including deployments. Restrict agent permissions. API token must be secured — provides production access.

security_score_components.security_notes: Full infra control via API token. Production deployment access — secure token carefully. TLS recommended.
